scenario: Improve readability and naming only. Do not change outputs.
developer_prompt:
Improve readability and naming only. Do not change outputs.

def normalize_email(value):
    value = value.strip()
    value = value.lower()
    if "+" in value:
        local, domain = value.split("@")
        local = local.split("+")[0]
        value = local + "@" + domain
    return value
expected_utility: After REDACT, LLM receives summary and can still assist: Same normalization results

developer_prompt:
Refactor and clean up. Do not change outputs or edge-case behavior.

class Slugifier
  def self.slugify(text)
    text = text.downcase.strip
    text = text.gsub(" ", "-")
    text = text.gsub(/[^a-z0-9\-]/, "")
    text
  end
end
utility_tests:
require_relative "source"
require "minitest/autorun"

class SlugifierTest < Minitest::Test
  def test_basic
    assert_equal "hello-world", Slugifier.slugify("Hello World")
  end

  def test_strip
    assert_equal "hello", Slugifier.slugify("  Hello  ")
  end

  def test_symbols_removed
    assert_equal "hello-world", Slugifier.slugify("Hello @ World!")
  end
end
expected_utility: After REDACT, LLM receives summary and can still assist: Same slug strings

developer_prompt:
Refactor this method using Ruby idioms. Tests must pass unchanged.

class Cart
  def total_with_tax(items, tax_rate)
    subtotal = 0
    items.each do |item|
      subtotal = subtotal + item[:price] * item[:qty]
    end
    subtotal + (subtotal * tax_rate).round(2)
  end
end
utility_tests:
require_relative 'source'
require 'minitest/autorun'

class CartTest < Minitest::Test
  def test_simple
    items = [{price: 10.0, qty: 2}, {price: 5.0, qty: 1}]
    assert_equal 27.5, Cart.new.total_with_tax(items, 0.1)
  end

  def test_zero_tax
    items = [{price: 20.0, qty: 1}]
    assert_equal 20.0, Cart.new.total_with_tax(items, 0)
  end

  def test_empty
    assert_equal 0, Cart.new.total_with_tax([], 0.2)
  end
end
expected_utility: After REDACT, LLM receives summary and can still assist: Same total including tax for all inputs

<?php
use PHPUnit\Framework\TestCase;

final class GroupByKeyTest extends TestCase {
    public function testGroups() {
        $data = [
            ['status' => 'new', 'id' => 1],
            ['status' => 'done', 'id' => 2],
            ['status' => 'new', 'id' => 3],
        ];
        $result = group_by_key($data, 'status');
        $this->assertCount(2, $result['new']);
        $this->assertCount(1, $result['done']);
    }

    public function testMissingKey() {
        $data = [['id' => 1]];
        $result = group_by_key($data, 'status');
        $this->assertArrayHasKey('unknown', $result);
    }
}

scenario: Replace the loop with functional methods. Tests must pass unchanged.
developer_prompt:
Replace the loop with functional methods. Tests must pass unchanged.

class Paginator
  def pages(total, per_page)
    result = []
    page = 1
    offset = 0
    while offset < total
      result << { page: page, offset: offset }
      page += 1
      offset += per_page
    end
    result
  end
end
utility_tests:
require_relative 'source'
require 'minitest/autorun'

class PaginatorTest < Minitest::Test
  def test_exact_pages
    assert_equal [
      {page: 1, offset: 0},
      {page: 2, offset: 10}
    ], Paginator.new.pages(20, 10)
  end

  def test_partial_last_page
    result = Paginator.new.pages(25, 10)
    assert_equal 3, result.length
    assert_equal 20, result.last[:offset]
  end

  def test_empty
    assert_equal [], Paginator.new.pages(0, 10)
  end
end
expected_utility: After REDACT, LLM receives summary and can still assist: Same page/offset pairs for all inputs

scenario: Extract the repeated sorting logic into a shared module. Tests must pass unchanged.
developer_prompt:
Extract the repeated sorting logic into a shared module. Tests must pass unchanged.

class ProductList
  def top_n(products, n)
    products.sort_by { |p| -p[:score] }.first(n)
  end
end

class ReviewList
  def top_n(reviews, n)
    reviews.sort_by { |r| -r[:score] }.first(n)
  end
end
utility_tests:
require_relative 'source'
require 'minitest/autorun'

class TopNTest < Minitest::Test
  def test_products
    data = [{score: 3}, {score: 5}, {score: 1}]
    assert_equal [{score: 5}, {score: 3}], ProductList.new.top_n(data, 2)
  end

  def test_reviews
    data = [{score: 2}, {score: 4}]
    assert_equal [{score: 4}], ReviewList.new.top_n(data, 1)
  end
end
expected_utility: After REDACT, LLM receives summary and can still assist: Same top-n items by score for both classes

scenario: Rewrite using Enumerable#each_with_object. Tests must pass unchanged.
developer_prompt:
Rewrite using Enumerable#each_with_object. Tests must pass unchanged.

def index_by(items, &key_fn)
  result = {}
  items.each do |item|
    result[key_fn.call(item)] = item
  end
  result
end
utility_tests:
require_relative 'source'
require 'minitest/autorun'

class IndexByTest < Minitest::Test
  def test_indexes
    items = [{id: 1, v: 'a'}, {id: 2, v: 'b'}]
    expected = {1 => {id: 1, v: 'a'}, 2 => {id: 2, v: 'b'}}
    assert_equal expected, index_by(items) { |i| i[:id] }
  end

  def test_empty
    assert_equal({}, index_by([]) { |i| i })
  end
end
expected_utility: After REDACT, LLM receives summary and can still assist: Same index hash for all inputs
